# Rules index

Generated from rule front-matter by `scripts/sync-index.mjs`. Always load `core` rules; load others
when their triggers/applies-to match (see `context-loading.md`).

| Rule | TL;DR | Load | Triggers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [rule:agent-delegation](agent-delegation.md) | Pick the right model per task (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku); frame each task + subagent with a task-fit senior persona; subagents inherit + follow the governance. | core | agent, subagent, delegation, model, spawn, task |
| [rule:automation](automation.md) | Local-first: check:all runs pre-commit + pre-push and nothing unproven is pushed; CI is a backstop that mirrors the same gate and blocks a red merge to main. | conditional | ci, automation, workflow, pipeline, merge, gate, hooks, pre-commit, pre-push |
| [rule:clarify-and-plan](clarify-and-plan.md) | Non-trivial work: plan first, surface unknowns, and resolve ambiguous decisions with the maintainer (AskUserQuestion) before coding — never guess a decision. | core | plan, clarify, ambiguous, decision, question, requirement, unknown, ask, scope |
| [rule:code-quality](code-quality.md) | Fix on sight; typed errors, no silent catches; small focused modules; strict lint/format; no dead code; never weaken a gate you do not own. | conditional | quality, error, lint, refactor, clean, unused, dead-code, config, overlay, gate |
| [rule:context-loading](context-loading.md) | Boot: CLAUDE.md + TLDR indexes + core docs only. Per task: load full docs whose tldr/triggers/applies-to match (or via context-for.mjs). Reload after compact. | core | context, loading, tldr, manifest, compact, resume |
| [rule:core-principles](core-principles.md) | Best solution only, verify everything, repo SSOT, reuse, security & tests, log everything, one-pass, fix don't remove, hand the knowledge over. | core | principle, start, overview |
| [rule:dependencies](dependencies.md) | Before adding a dependency: justify it, check license + maintenance health, prefer few/small deps; pin exact versions + commit lockfiles so builds never drift. | conditional | dependency, dependencies, package, add, license, supply-chain, vendor, lockfile, cve, advisory |
| [rule:documentation](documentation.md) | Every exported boundary item carries a doc comment (purpose, inputs/outputs, failure modes); comments explain intent, not mechanics; docs travel with code. | conditional | doc, documentation, comment, docstring, api-docs |
| [rule:editing-workflow](editing-workflow.md) | Each change ships code + tests + docs/ADR + green check:all as one Conventional Commit; run governance:sync after touching governance docs. | conditional | workflow, commit, edit, change, pr |
| [rule:git-workflow](git-workflow.md) | The maintainer alone decides branches: the agent never creates, switches, merges or deletes one without explicit instruction; no force-push, no --no-verify. | core | git, branch, checkout, merge, rebase, push, commit, worktree, workflow |
| [rule:knowledge-handover](knowledge-handover.md) | Knowledge must reach the next agent: enforce new mechanisms in the gate, place them where that agent loads them, and prove reachability (context-for.mjs). | core | handover, knowledge, onboarding, agent, mechanism, policy, discoverable, reachable, brief |
| [rule:logging](logging.md) | Every component logs action + result through one structured logger; log the lifecycle and every error with context; never log secrets; no silent failures. | conditional | logging, observability, error, debug, logs, structured, levels |
| [rule:no-guessing](no-guessing.md) | Never invent APIs, versions, file shapes or behaviours; read the actual source/schema/data first, verify a version against its registry, cite what you claim. | conditional | guess, assume, api, version, unknown, invent, hallucination |
| [rule:privacy](privacy.md) | No telemetry, analytics, crash-reporting or phone-home: the app sends nothing off-device without an explicit, opt-in, ADR-backed feature and user consent. | conditional | privacy, telemetry, analytics, tracking, egress, network, consent, phone-home, crash-report |
| [rule:response-format](response-format.md) | Be concise and evidence-led; cite file:line; state what was verified vs assumed; keep docs/ADRs/tests updated within the same change. | core | response, format, communication, output |
| [rule:reusability](reusability.md) | Before adding code, search for an existing util/component to reuse or extend; one source per cross-cutting concern; a justified second copy needs an ADR. | conditional | reuse, duplicate, component, shared, dry, ssot, copy |
| [rule:rule-maintenance](rule-maintenance.md) | New policy -> a rule/ADR with full front-matter, in the right layer (never edit an upstream one); regenerate indexes; keep tldrs accurate and the gate green. | conditional | rule, adr, maintenance, policy, index, layer, project-line, supersede |
| [rule:security](security.md) | Validate every input at the trust boundary; least privilege; secrets in the OS keyring, never in logs or a client; HTTPS + timeouts; advisories block the push. | conditional | security, secret, redaction, capability, permission, network, path, boundary, threat |
| [rule:testing](testing.md) | Test-first (TDD): the failing test comes before the code; unit tests per module, contracts pinned both sides, filesystem work in a temp dir; coverage gated. | conditional | test, testing, coverage, fixture, tdd, unit, integration, contract |
| [rule:verification](verification.md) | Prove correctness: tests per module, exercise the real thing end-to-end, show the evidence (command + output); check:all green before declaring done. | conditional | verify, proof, evidence, check, done, prove |
| [rule:versioning](versioning.md) | Every landing change bumps SemVer by its commit type (npm version --no-git-tag-version); releasing/tagging only on the maintainer's word; package.json is SSOT. | conditional | version, release, bump, semver, changelog, tag, released, reopen |
